references.title: Group formation based on reading annotation data: system innovation and classroom practice

annotations.text: The work proposes a different way of grouping students for collaborative learning taking into consideration learning logs and reading annotations. The approach was field-tested on a set of high-school students. Results show that students who were part of the heteroogenous groups not only performed better but were seen to perceive more effort on the part of their group. Authors propose new opportunities for group formation implementations.
